nodes.title: Clone arrays with `slice()`
nodes.why: `slice()` makes a new top-level array while keeping the function short and familiar.
nodes.tradeoff: Very readable for arrays, but it is still only a shallow copy.

nodes.title: Clone plain objects with spread
nodes.why: Object spread is the simplest way to copy top-level enumerable properties.
nodes.tradeoff: Compact and modern syntax, but nested values still stay shared by reference.

nodes.title: Throw for values that are not objects or arrays

anchor.snippet: throw new TypeError('Expected object or array');

nodes.why: The helper stays honest about what it supports instead of returning misleading output for primitives.
nodes.alternative: Return the value unchanged for primitives.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter behavior, but easier to debug when the caller passes the wrong type.
